Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Activate test: next it.todo (2 runes + 1 moonstone → premium 88)
Current state: 10 tests passing
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by loading the shared context files and reading the current state.

1	# MHPCO Claim Office — Example Mapping
2	
3	Shared spec memory for Red/Green subagents. Source of truth for field
4	names, rule application order, and expected numeric values.
5	
6	## Rules
7	
8	### R1. Item base values and premiums
9	Main items have fixed insurance value and base premium:
10	- sword: 1000 G / 100 G
11	- amulet: 600 G / 60 G
12	- staff: 800 G / 80 G
13	- potion: 400 G / 40 G
14	
15	Components (rune, moonstone): 250 G insurance value / 25 G base premium each.
16	
17	### R2. Component "building block" discount
18	A group of exactly 3 alike (same type) components is offered as a block at
19	60 G base premium instead of 75 G. The block discount affects the premium
20	only, NOT the insurance sum. Greedy grouping by type.
21	
22	### R3. Item-level premium modifiers
23	Applied to each affected item's base premium (additive percentages of the
24	item's own base premium):
25	- cursed: +50 %
26	- enchantment ≥ 5: +30 %
27	
28	### R4. Policy-level premium modifiers
29	Applied to the policy base premium (sum of item base premiums after R2):
30	- yearsWithMHPCO ≥ 2: −20 % loyalty discount
31	- first insurance: +10 % initial assessment (applies to EACH item in EACH
32	  quote regardless of customer history — see ❓Q3)
33	- follow-up contract (every quote after the customer's first one in this
34	  scenario): −15 %
35	
36	### R5. Processing fee
37	+5 G flat fee, added at the very end after all other modifiers and rounding.
38	
39	### R6. Rounding in MHPCO's favor
40	- Premiums: rounded UP to whole G
41	- Payouts: rounded DOWN to whole G
42	- Intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only the final amount rounded
43	
44	### R7. Insurance sum and cap
45	Insurance sum = sum of items' insurance values (unmodified, block does
46	NOT reduce). Cap = 2 × insurance sum, based on UNMODIFIED insurance
47	values (premium modifiers don't raise cap).
48	
49	### R8. Claim deductible
50	100 G deductible per damage event (per item entry in damages array).
51	
52	### R9. Claim clauses (per item)
53	- enchantment ≥ 8: reimbursed at 50 % of damage (then deductible)
54	- dragon material: full reimbursement (then deductible)
55	- If both apply: 50 % wins, then deductible (per example: dragon sword
56	  ench 9, dmg 1000 → 500 − 100 = 400)
57	- Components (no enchantment/material): full reimbursement minus
58	  deductible
59	
60	### R10. Cap exhaustion across claims
61	Payouts are capped by the remaining cap. If desired payout exceeds
62	remaining cap, it's reduced to the remaining cap. Cap depletes across
63	successive claims on the same policy.
64	
65	### R11. Error cases (CLI exits non-zero, writes to stderr, no results
66	on stdout)
67	- Unknown item type in quote
68	- Claim damage references item not in policy (or unknown type)
69	- Damages array has more entries of a type than policy covers
70	- Negative damage amount
71	
72	## Examples
73	
74	### Item base premiums (R1)
75	- sword: base 100 G
76	- amulet: base 60 G
77	- staff: base 80 G
78	- potion: base 40 G
79	- 1 rune: base 25 G
80	
81	### Building blocks (R2)
82	- 2 runes → 50 G base premium
83	- 3 runes → 60 G base premium (block applies)
84	- 4 runes → 100 G base premium (no block — block requires exactly 3)
85	- 7 runes → 175 G (one block of 3 + 4 individual = 60 + 100)
86	  — wait: spec says 7 runes → 175 G. 3+3+1 = 60+60+25 = 145 G? Or
87	  3+4 = 60+100 = 160 G? Or 7×25 = 175 G (no block applied)? Spec
88	  literally says "175 G" — that's 7 × 25, so blocks do NOT auto-apply
89	  greedily on 7. Re-reading: "3 alike components is offered at a
90	  special base premium of 60 G" — must be exactly 3 to qualify.
91	  Therefore 7 = no block → 175 G. (See ❓Q4 for resolution.)
92	
93	### "Alike" components (R2, ❓Q1)
94	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 75 G (no block: different types, 3×25 = 75)
95	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G (two separate blocks: 60 + 60)
96	
97	### Modifier scope (R3, R4)
98	- cursed sword + plain amulet:
99	  - policy base premium = 100 + 60 = 160 G
100	  - cursed surcharge = 50 G (50 % of cursed sword's base, not policy)
101	  - subtotal = 210 G before further modifiers and fee
102	
103	### Modifier thresholds
104	- exactly 2 years → loyalty applies
105	- exactly enchantment 5 → high-enchantment surcharge applies
106	- enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment surcharge
107	- if cursed AND enchantment ≥ 5 → both surcharges apply
108	- dragon-material sword, enchantment exactly 8, dmg 1000 G → payout 400 G
109	  (high-enchantment 50 % rule wins, then deductible)
110	
111	### Claim — multi-item deductible (R8)
112	- sword damage 500 G + amulet damage 300 G in same incident
113	  → payout = (500 − 100) + (300 − 100) = 600 G
114	
115	### Standard reimbursement (R9)
116	- regular sword (steel, ench 3), dmg 500 G → payout 400 G
117	- rune (insurance 250 G), dmg 200 G → payout 100 G
118	
119	### Enchantment vs. dragon material (R9)
120	- dragon sword, ench 9, dmg 1000 G → 400 G (50 % rule + deductible)
121	- dragon sword, ench 5, dmg 800 G → 700 G (dragon only: full − 100)
122	- steel sword, ench 9, dmg 1000 G → 400 G (50 % + deductible)
123	
124	### Multiple items of same type (❓Q2)
125	- two swords policy → insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G
126	- two sword damage entries → each treated separately with own deductible
127	- more sword damages than swords insured → CLI exits non-zero, claim rejected
128	
129	### Cap exhaustion (R7, R10)
130	- sword + amulet policy → insurance sum 1600 G, cap 3200 G
131	- cursed sword (premium with mods 165 G) → cap 2000 G (unmodified value)
132	- sword + 3 runes (block) → insurance sum 1750 G (= 1000 + 3×250),
133	  cap 3500 G (block doesn't affect insurance sum)
134	- sword cap 2000 G; two claims of 1500 G each:
135	  - claim 1 → desired 1500 − 100 = 1400 G; cap remaining 600 G
136	  - claim 2 → desired 1400 G reduced to 600 G; cap remaining 0 G
137	
138	### Rounding (R6)
139	- premium 197.5 G → 198 G (up)
140	- payout 350.5 G → 350 G (down)
141	
142	### Edge cases
143	- empty item list → premium 5 G (only processing fee)
144	- unknown item type in quote (e.g. broomstick) → CLI exits non-zero,
145	  error to stderr, no results
146	- claim with item not in policy → CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
147	- claim with negative amount → CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
148	
149	### Integration: Newcomer with cursed sword
150	- customer 0 years, no previous contracts
151	- cursed sword (steel, ench 3)
152	- premium 165 G = 100 (base) + 50 (curse) + 10 (first insurance)
153	  = 160 + 5 fee = 165 G
154	- (no loyalty since 0 years; no follow-up since first quote)
155	
156	### Integration: Long-standing customer, second contract
157	- customer 3 years, second quote in scenario
158	- cursed sword (steel, ench 7)
159	- premium 160 G:
160	  - 100 (base) + 50 (curse) + 30 (high ench) = 180 item subtotal
161	  - −20 (loyalty 20 % of 100 base = 20) → wait spec breakdown:
162	    "100 + 50 + 30 − 20 + 10 − 15 = 155 + 5 = 160"
163	  - So loyalty 20 = 20 % of 100 G policy base premium = 20 G
164	  - first insurance 10 = 10 % of 100 G policy base = 10 G
165	  - follow-up 15 = 15 % of 100 G policy base = 15 G
166	  - Policy-level modifiers (loyalty, first-insurance, follow-up) are
167	    each computed as percentage of the POLICY base premium (sum of
168	    items' unmodified base premiums), NOT of the modified subtotal.
169	  - Total: 100 + 50 + 30 − 20 + 10 − 15 = 155 → +5 fee = 160 G
170	
171	## Interface contract
172	
173	### CLI: `src/cli.ts`
174	Reads ONE JSON document from stdin, writes ONE JSON document to stdout.
175	On error: exits non-zero, writes error description to stderr, NO
176	`results` written to stdout.
177	
178	### Input shape (verbatim field names)
179	```json
180	{
181	  "customer": {"yearsWithMHPCO": 5},
182	  "steps": [
183	    {
184	      "op": "quote",
185	      "items": [
186	        {"type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false}
187	      ]
188	    },
189	    {
190	      "op": "claim",
191	      "policy": 0,
192	      "incident": {
193	        "cause": "fire",
194	        "damages": [
195	          {"itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200}
196	        ]
197	      }
198	    }
199	  ]
200	}
201	```
202	
203	Item types: `sword`, `amulet`, `staff`, `potion`, `rune`, `moonstone`.
204	Item fields: `type` (required), `material` (optional string),
205	`enchantment` (optional integer, default 0), `cursed` (optional boolean,
206	default false).
207	
208	### Output shape (verbatim field names)
209	```json
210	{
211	  "results": [
212	    {"premium": 71},
213	    {"payout": 100, "remainingCap": 1100}
214	  ]
215	}
216	```
217	
218	- Quote result: `{"premium": <integer>}`
219	- Claim result: `{"payout": <integer>, "remainingCap": <integer>}`
220	- `results` length and order match input `steps`.
221	
222	### Policy reference
223	`claim.policy` is the zero-based index into `steps` of the `quote` step
224	that created the policy.
225	
226	## Questions / Clarifications
227	
228	- **Q1 (alike components)** — Resolved: "alike" means same type. Runes
229	  and moonstones are different types (don't combine into one block).
230	- **Q2 (multiple items same type)** — Resolved: each entry in `items` is
231	  a separate insured item; each entry in `damages` is a separate damage
232	  event; mismatch (more damages of a type than insured) → reject claim
233	  with non-zero exit.
234	- **Q3 (scope of "first insurance")** — Resolved: each item in each
235	  quote is treated as a first insurance (the +10 % surcharge always
236	  applies per item). Follow-up discount (−15 %) applies to the whole
237	  quote when it's not the customer's first quote in this scenario.
238	- **Q4 (modifier scope)** — Resolved: item-specific modifiers (cursed,
239	  high enchantment, first insurance) apply per item to that item's
240	  base premium; policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, follow-up) apply as a
241	  percentage of the policy base premium (sum of items' unmodified base
242	  premiums). Processing fee added last.
243	
244	  Refining from the integration breakdown: loyalty −20, first-insurance
245	  +10, follow-up −15 are each `% × 100 (single-item policy base)`. With
246	  one item the distinction between "per item" and "per policy" doesn't
247	  bite, but the spec text explicitly says first-insurance is per item
248	  (each item in a quote is a first insurance), while loyalty and
249	  follow-up are policy-wide.
250	
251	  Formula:
252	  ```
253	  for each item:
254	    item_subtotal = base × (1 + 0.5·cursed + 0.3·highEnch + 0.1·firstIns)
255	  policy_subtotal = Σ item_subtotal
256	  policy_base    = Σ item_base  (unmodified)
257	  policy_after_policy_mods =
258	    policy_subtotal − 0.20·policy_base·(years≥2) − 0.15·policy_base·(followUp)
259	  final = ceil(policy_after_policy_mods) + 5
260	  ```
261	  Verifies integration example 1 (newcomer): 100×(1+0.5+0.1)=160; no
262	  policy mods; 160+5=165 ✓
263	  Verifies integration example 2: 100×(1+0.5+0.3+0.1)=190;
264	  policy mods: −20 (loyalty) −15 (follow-up) = −35 → 155; +5 = 160 ✓
265	
266	## Per-test rationale
267	
268	Each `it.todo()` below maps to one rule or example:
269	
270	### Quote operation — function-level (via scenario interface)
271	01. empty items, 0 years → premium 5 (R5 only) — edge case
272	02. one sword, 0 years → premium 116 (100 base + 10 first-ins + 5 fee
273	    = 110×1 = wait: 100 + 10 = 110, +5 fee = 115. Recompute: item
274	    subtotal = 100×1.1 = 110; no policy mods; 110+5 = 115). So
275	    premium 115 — R1, R5, R4-firstIns
276	03. one amulet, 0 years → premium 71 (60×1.1=66, +5=71) — R1
277	04. one staff → premium 93 (80×1.1=88, +5=93) — R1
278	05. one potion → premium 49 (40×1.1=44, +5=49) — R1
279	06. one rune → premium 33 (25×1.1=27.5 → ceil 28, +5=33) — R1, R6
280	07. 2 runes → 55 G (no block: 50×1.1=55, +5=60) — R2
281	08. 3 runes → block: (60×1.1=66, +5=71) — R2
282	09. 4 runes → no block: (100×1.1=110, +5=115) — R2
283	10. 7 runes → 175 base: 175×1.1=192.5 → ceil 193, +5=198 — R2,R6
284	11. 2 runes + 1 moonstone → no block: 75×1.1=82.5→ceil 83, +5=88 — R2,Q1
285	12. 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 2 blocks: 120×1.1=132,+5=137 — R2,Q1
286	13. cursed sword (0 years) → 165 (integration ex 1) — R3,R5
287	14. sword enchantment 5 (0 years) → 100×(1+0.3+0.1)=140, +5=145 — R3
288	15. sword enchantment 4 (0 years) → 100×(1+0.1)=110, +5=115 — R3 threshold
289	16. cursed sword enchantment 5 → 100×(1+0.5+0.3+0.1)=190,+5=195 — R3
290	17. 2 years customer, one sword → 100×1.1 − 0.2×100 + 5 = 110−20+5=95 — R4
291	18. 3 years customer, second quote, cursed ench 7 sword → 160 (integration ex 2)
292	    — R3, R4 all combined
293	19. cursed sword + plain amulet (0 years, first quote): item subtotals
294	    100×(1+0.5+0.1)=160, 60×(1+0.1)=66; total 226; no policy mods;
295	    +5=231 — R3 scope (cursed adds 50 only to sword)
296	20. rounding up: scenario producing 197.5 → 198 (e.g. 7 runes case
297	    covers this; or design explicit: 7 runes gives 198) — R6
298	21. customer exactly 2 years (boundary) → loyalty applies — R4 threshold
299	
300	### Claim operation
301	22. sword policy (1000 G insurance, cap 2000), damage 500 G regular →
302	    payout 400, remaining 1600 — R7, R8, R9
303	23. rune policy (250 G insurance), damage 200 G → payout 100, remaining 400
304	    — R8, R9 (components no clauses)
305	24. sword + amulet policy, dragon damages both (sword 500, amulet 300)
306	    → payout 600 (400+200), remaining 2600 — R8 multi-item
307	25. dragon sword ench 8, dmg 1000 → payout 400 (50% wins, then deductible)
308	    — R9 boundary
309	26. dragon sword ench 9, dmg 1000 → payout 400 — R9
310	27. dragon sword ench 5, dmg 800 → payout 700 (dragon only) — R9
311	28. steel sword ench 9, dmg 1000 → payout 400 (high-ench only) — R9
312	29. two swords policy, dragon damages both swords → each separate
313	    deductible (e.g. 500+500 → payout 800, cap 4000−800=3200) — Q2
314	30. two sword damage entries but only one sword insured → CLI exits
315	    non-zero, error on stderr, no results — Q2 error
316	31. cap exhaustion: sword policy (cap 2000), two claims of 1500 each:
317	    - claim 1: desired 1400, payout 1400, remaining 600
318	    - claim 2: desired 1400 reduced to 600, payout 600, remaining 0
319	    — R10
320	32. cursed sword cap = 2000 G (not based on premium modifiers) — R7
321	33. sword + 3 runes (block) → insurance sum 1750, cap 3500 — R7
322	
323	### Payout rounding (R6)
324	34. payout that yields 350.5 → 350 (round down). Example: dragon sword
325	    ench 8, dmg 901 → 901/2 − 100 = 350.5 → 350
326	
327	### CLI / error cases
328	35. CLI: reads JSON from stdin, writes results JSON to stdout (schema
329	    example: amulet 5-year quote then claim) — interface contract
330	36. CLI: quote with unknown item type (broomstick) → exit non-zero,
331	    error to stderr, no results on stdout
332	37. CLI: claim references item not in policy → exit non-zero, error
333	    to stderr
334	38. CLI: claim with negative amount → exit non-zero, error to stderr
335	39. CLI: multi-step scenario with quote followed by claim referencing
336	    policy by zero-based index → results array matches steps order
337

content.text:
Now I understand the state. Test #11 is "2 runes + 1 moonstone → premium 88". The current implementation only handles all-runes; for a mixed list with `rune` and `moonstone`, `items.every(isRune)` is false, so it falls through to `PREMIUM_FOR_SINGLE_ITEM[items[0].type]` which is `PREMIUM_FOR_SINGLE_ITEM["rune"]` = `undefined`.

Let me activate the test.

input.old_string: it.todo("2 runes + 1 moonstone yields base 75 (no block: different types), premium 88 (75*1.10=82.5→83, +5)");

content.text:
Now make compilation prediction.

Red Phase - Compilation Error Prediction:
- Test: "2 runes + 1 moonstone yields base 75 (no block: different types), premium 88"
- Expected: No compilation error (function `runScenario` already exists; types match prior tests; `{type: "moonstone"}` matches the `Item` type which is just `{type: string}`)
- Reason: Function exists from prior cycles; no new symbols required.
- Error: N/A — expect compilation to succeed and proceed directly to runtime/assertion failure.

Red Phase - Runtime Error Prediction:
- Test: "2 runes + 1 moonstone yields base 75 (no block: different types), premium 88"
- Expected: Runtime assertion error
- Expected value: `{ results: [{ premium: 88 }] }`
- Actual value: `{ results: [{ premium: undefined }] }` (items.every(isRune) is false; falls into PREMIUM_FOR_SINGLE_ITEM["rune"] which is undefined)
- Diff:
  ```
  Expected: { results: [{ premium: 88 }] }
  Received: { results: [{ premium: undefined }] }
  ```

Now run the test.

Test fails as predicted. Both predictions held.

Now append journal entry.

input.old_string: 10 G | branch on length==7 && all runes -> 198 | passing:10
input.new_string:
10 G | branch on length==7 && all runes -> 198 | passing:10
11 R | 2 runes + 1 moonstone -> premium 88 (no block, different types) | comp:OK | run:OK | comp N/A (fn exists); runtime predicted Expected 88 Received undefined

content.text:
Red Phase Complete:
**Test Activated**: "2 runes + 1 moonstone yields base 75 (no block: different types), premium 88 (75*1.10=82.5→83, +5)"
**Compilation Prediction**: No compilation error expected (function and types already exist from prior cycles) Correct
**Runtime Prediction**: Expected `{ results: [{ premium: 88 }] }`, received `{ results: [{ premium: undefined }] }` Correct
**Result**: Test fails with AssertionError as expected — `items.every(isRune)` is false (moonstone present), so fallthrough returns `PREMIUM_FOR_SINGLE_ITEM["rune"]` which is `undefined`.
